notes: Static synthetic content to unblock Tier-2 Playwright specs for Search v3.
The build-script --search-slice mode captures these same shapes from a real corpus + running API when the operator regenerates locally (see README.md).
Anchored to episode_ids present in the parent fixture's manifest.json so hit-card handoffs to Library / Graph resolve cleanly.
For the shape contract see RFC-107 §2 (SearchResponse), RFC-072 §6 (lifted compound), RFC-088 chunk 5 (enriched-answer), ADR-108 (topic_consensus tuple).

anchor_feeds.note: 5 episodes on this feed — used for Cluster (theme members) + Consensus (cross-speaker pairs) scenarios

compound-lift.description: Transcript hit that lifts to a linked GI Insight (RFC-072 KL1 shipped). WorkspaceResults renders this as a CompoundCard with a '+ insight' badge (UXS-016 §Results).

results.text: The question of who governs compute — the actual silicon and the datacenters that host it — is being answered right now, not by regulators.

insight.text: Compute governance is being determined by private-sector datacenter siting decisions rather than public regulation.

results.text: The concentration of AI-relevant fab capacity in Taiwan makes silicon supply a national-security concern for every G7.

metadata.episode_title: BlackRock's Rob Goldstein on the New Rules

enriched-answer.description: Enriched-answer hero visible when /api/health reports enriched_search_available AND request has enrich_results=true (RFC-088 chunk 5, UXS-016 §EnrichedAnswerHero). Sources link back to hit cards via doc_id.

params.q: why does Taiwan matter to AI compute

response.query: why does Taiwan matter to AI compute

enriched.answer: Across the Odd Lots corpus, guests converge on a single frame: Taiwan holds the concentrated fab capacity that AI-relevant chips depend on, so any disruption to that capacity is a first-order risk for compute supply. Rob Goldstein frames it as a national-security concern for every G7. Tracy Alloway extends the argument to compute governance itself — private-sector datacenter siting decisions are shaping the practical envelope of AI development faster than public regulation is.

operator-cluster.description: Result-set operator Cluster (RFC-107 §6.1, UXS-016 §Operators). Server groups hits via insight_clusters / theme_clusters into named clusters with ≥5 members. Client renders ClusterGroupCard.

operator-consensus.description: Result-set operator Consensus (RFC-107 §6.5, UXS-016 §Operators, ADR-108). Surfaces topic_consensus enricher output — cross-speaker corroboration pairs, NOT contradictions (typed CONTRADICTS is v3+, out of scope). Pair tuple shape per RFC-088 §Reimagining note: (topic_id, person_a_id, person_b_id, insight_a_id, insight_b_id, contradiction_score). The 'contradiction_score' name is legacy; semantically it is the composite consensus signal per ADR-108 (low score + high embedding cosine ⇒ agreement).

pairs.insight_a_text: Regulation will materially lag the pace of frontier-model deployment for at least 24 months.
pairs.insight_b_text: By the time meaningful AI rules land, the deployed system will have already trained the next generation.

pairs.insight_a_text: The actual envelope of compute is set by the firms deciding where to put the next datacenter, not by any regulator.
pairs.insight_b_text: Compute governance is being determined by private-sector datacenter siting decisions rather than public regulation.

temporal-intent.description: Query the router classifies as temporal_tracking (RFC-092 taxonomy). Header IntentChip renders 'Temporal tracking' (UXS-016 §Header §Intent row). Timeline operator would bucket these hits by publish_date.

params.q: how has thinking on AI compute governance evolved over the last year

response.query: how has thinking on AI compute governance evolved over the last year

results.text: Early in the year the frame was still: public infrastructure decisions will shape the envelope of AI compute.
By late spring the frame has flipped — private siting decisions are already setting the envelope.
